name: convnext

members.name: ConvNeXtConfig
members.signature: ConvNeXtConfig(num_classes: int = 1000, in_channels: int = 3, depths: tuple[int, ...] = (3, 3, 9, 3), dims: tuple[int, ...] = (96, 192, 384, 768), layer_scale_init: float = 1e-06, layer_norm_eps: float = 1e-06, dropout: float = 0.0)

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(num_classes: int = 1000, in_channels: int = 3, depths: tuple[int, ...] = (3, 3, 9, 3), dims: tuple[int, ...] = (96, 192, 384, 768), layer_scale_init: float = 1e-06, layer_norm_eps: float = 1e-06, dropout: float = 0.0)

members.summary: Configuration dataclass for every ConvNeXt variant.
members.extended:
`ConvNeXtConfig` is an immutable container that fully specifies
the architecture of a ConvNeXt.  It is consumed by both
`ConvNeXt` (backbone) and
`ConvNeXtForImageClassification` (classifier).  All five
canonical variants described in [Liu et al. (2022)](https://arxiv.org/abs/2201.03545) — Tiny / Small /
Base / Large / XLarge — can be expressed by choosing different
`depths` and `dims`.

Each ConvNeXt block applies a depthwise $7 \times 7$
convolution followed by a LayerNorm and an inverted-bottleneck MLP,
with a learnable per-channel *layer scale* $\gamma$ on the
residual branch:

$$
x \leftarrow x + \gamma \odot
\mathrm{Linear}_{4d \to d}\!\bigl(
    \mathrm{GELU}\!\bigl(\mathrm{Linear}_{d \to 4d}\!\bigl(
    \mathrm{LN}(\mathrm{DWConv}_{7 \times 7}(x))\bigr)\bigr)\bigr).
$$

parameters.name: num_classes
parameters.description:
Number of output classes for the classification head.  Defaults
to `1000` (ImageNet-1k).

parameters.name: in_channels
parameters.description: Number of input image channels.  Defaults to `3` (RGB).

parameters.name: depths
parameters.description:
Number of ConvNeXt blocks in each of the four stages.  Defaults
to `(3, 3, 9, 3)` (ConvNeXt-T).

parameters.name: dims
parameters.description:
Output channel width of each of the four stages.  Defaults to
`(96, 192, 384, 768)` (ConvNeXt-T).

parameters.name: layer_scale_init
parameters.description:
Initial value $\gamma_0$ of the per-channel layer-scale
parameter applied to every residual branch.  Defaults to
`1e-6` (Touvron et al., 2021).

parameters.name: dropout
parameters.description:
Maximum stochastic-depth rate; linearly scheduled across all
blocks of the trunk (Liu et al., 2022, §3).  Defaults to
`0.0`.

examples:
Build a ConvNeXt-T configuration for CIFAR-10 (10 classes):
>>> from lucid.models.vision.convnext import ConvNeXtConfig
>>> cfg = ConvNeXtConfig(num_classes=10)
>>> cfg.depths, cfg.dims, cfg.num_classes
((3, 3, 9, 3), (96, 192, 384, 768), 10)

attributes.name: model_type
attributes.description: Constant string `"convnext"` used by the model registry.

members.name: ConvNeXt
members.extended:
ConvNeXt is a *pure-convolutional* backbone built by systematically
modernising a ResNet using design choices borrowed from the Swin
Transformer.  The trunk is a four-stage pyramid that starts with a
non-overlapping $4 \times 4$ patchify stem and processes the
feature map through stacks of identical *ConvNeXt blocks*:

$$
x \leftarrow x + \gamma \odot
    \mathrm{Linear}_{4d \to d}\!\bigl(
    \mathrm{GELU}\!\bigl(\mathrm{Linear}_{d \to 4d}\!\bigl(
    \mathrm{LN}(\mathrm{DWConv}_{7 \times 7}(x))
    \bigr)\bigr)\bigr),
$$

where $\gamma \in \mathbb{R}^d$ is a per-channel *layer-scale*
parameter initialised to $10^{-6}$ (Touvron et al., 2021).
Between stages a $2 \times 2$ stride-2 LN + Conv2d downsampler
halves the resolution and doubles the channel width.

`forward_features` returns a global-average-pooled
$(B, C_{\text{out}})$ feature where
$C_{\text{out}} = \texttt{dims[-1]}$.  Use this backbone when
you want features for transfer learning or dense prediction; for
end-to-end classification use
`ConvNeXtForImageClassification`.

parameters.name: config
parameters.description:
Frozen dataclass specifying `depths`, `dims`,
`layer_scale_init`, `dropout`, and `in_channels`.  See
`ConvNeXtConfig`.

examples:
Build a ConvNeXt-T backbone and run a forward pass:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.convnext import ConvNeXt, ConvNeXtConfig
>>> model = ConvNeXt(ConvNeXtConfig())
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> feat = model.forward_features(x)
>>> feat.shape                       # (B, dims[-1])
(1, 768)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.last_hidden_state.shape      # (B, 1, dims[-1])
(1, 1, 768)
notes:
Reference: Zhuang Liu *et al.*, *"A ConvNet for the 2020s"*,
CVPR 2022, [arXiv:2201.03545](https://arxiv.org/abs/2201.03545).

attributes.name: stem
attributes.description:
Patchify stem: $4 \times 4$ stride-4 Conv2d followed by a
channel-last LayerNorm.

attributes.name: downsamplers
attributes.description: Three between-stage LN + stride-2 Conv2d downsamplers.

attributes.name: head_norm
attributes.description: Final LayerNorm applied (channel-last) to the pooled feature.

attributes.name: avgpool
attributes.description: $1 \times 1$ adaptive average pool over spatial dims.

attributes.name: feature_info
attributes.description:
Four-stage feature description with reductions
$(4, 8, 16, 32)$ and channel counts from `config.dims`.

members.name: ConvNeXtForImageClassification

methods.name: forward
methods.signature: forward(x: Tensor, labels: Tensor | None = None)

members.summary: ConvNeXt with a linear classification head (Liu et al., 2022).
members.extended:
Wraps the same four-stage trunk as `ConvNeXt` (patchify stem
→ four stages of ConvNeXt blocks with between-stage downsamplers)
and adds a global average pool, a final channel-last LayerNorm, and
a single `nn.Linear` classification head:

$$
\text{logits} = W_{\text{head}}\,
    \mathrm{LN}(\mathrm{GAP}(z^{L})) + b_{\text{head}},
\qquad W_{\text{head}} \in
    \mathbb{R}^{C_{\text{out}} \times d_{L}}.
$$

Pass `labels` to `forward` to compute the cross-entropy
loss in the same pass.

parameters.name: config
parameters.description:
Architecture specification.  Must set `num_classes` to the
desired number of output categories (default 1000 for
ImageNet-1k).  See `ConvNeXtConfig`.

examples:
End-to-end inference with the default ConvNeXt-T classifier:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.convnext import (
...     ConvNeXtConfig, ConvNeXtForImageClassification,
... )
>>> model = ConvNeXtForImageClassification(ConvNeXtConfig())
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape
(1, 1000)
notes:
Reference: Zhuang Liu *et al.*, *"A ConvNet for the 2020s"*,
CVPR 2022.  ConvNeXt-T / S / B / L / XL reach **82.1 / 83.1 /
83.8 / 84.3 / 85.5 % top-1 on ImageNet-1k** (Table 1 of the paper,
224x224 input) — matching or exceeding Swin at equal FLOPs.

attributes.name: stages
attributes.description: Four stages of stacked ConvNeXt blocks.

attributes.name: downsamplers
attributes.description: Three between-stage LN + stride-2 Conv2d downsamplers.

attributes.name: head_norm
attributes.description: Final LayerNorm applied to the pooled feature.

attributes.name: classifier
attributes.description: Final linear projection of width `(num_classes, dims[-1])`.

members.name: convnext_tiny
members.extended:
Builds the canonical *ConvNeXt-T* configuration: `depths=(3, 3, 9, 3)`,
`dims=(96, 192, 384, 768)`.  The final stage produces a
$(B, 768, H/32, W/32)$ feature map; `forward_features`
globally averages and LayerNorms to a $(B, 768)$ vector.
Approximately **29M parameters** — Swin-T equivalent.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
If `True`, loads ImageNet-1k pretrained weights when available
in the model zoo.  Defaults to `False`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Keyword overrides on top of the canonical ConvNeXt-T config.
Each override must match a field of `ConvNeXtConfig`.

returns.description:
A `ConvNeXt` backbone returning a flat
$(B, 768)$ feature.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.convnext import convnext_tiny
>>> model = convnext_tiny()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> feat = model.forward_features(x)
>>> feat.shape
(1, 768)
notes:
ConvNeXt-T reaches **82.1% top-1 on ImageNet-1k** (Liu et al., 2022,
Table 1).  See [arXiv:2201.03545](https://arxiv.org/abs/2201.03545).

members.name: convnext_tiny_cls
members.signature: convnext_tiny_cls(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: ConvNeXtTinyWeights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.extended:
Combines the `convnext_tiny` backbone with a global average
pool + LayerNorm + single `nn.Linear` classification head.
Default output is `num_classes=1000` (ImageNet-1k).  ~29M
parameters.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
If `True`, loads ImageNet-1k pretrained weights when
available.  Defaults to `False`.

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description: Explicit weights enum member; takes precedence over `pretrained`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides on top of the canonical ConvNeXt-T config.

returns.description:
Classifier returning `ImageClassificationOutput` whose
`logits` has shape `(B, num_classes)`.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.convnext import convnext_tiny_cls
>>> model = convnext_tiny_cls(num_classes=1000)
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> model(x).logits.shape
(1, 1000)
Load ImageNet-pretrained weights:
>>> model = convnext_tiny_cls(pretrained=True)            # DEFAULT tag
>>> from lucid.models.weights import ConvNeXtTinyWeights
>>> model = convnext_tiny_cls(weights=ConvNeXtTinyWeights.IMAGENET1K_V1)
notes:
ConvNeXt-T reaches **82.1% top-1 on ImageNet-1k** (Liu et al.,
2022, Table 1).

members.name: convnext_small
members.extended:
Builds the canonical *ConvNeXt-S* configuration: `depths=(3, 3, 27, 3)`
(3x deeper stage 3 than ConvNeXt-T), `dims=(96, 192, 384, 768)`.
Approximately **50M parameters** — Swin-S equivalent.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
If `True`, loads ImageNet-1k pretrained weights when available.
Defaults to `False`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides on top of the canonical ConvNeXt-S config.

returns.description:
A `ConvNeXt` backbone returning a flat $(B, 768)$
feature.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.convnext import convnext_small
>>> model = convnext_small()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> model.forward_features(x).shape
(1, 768)
notes:
ConvNeXt-S reaches **83.1% top-1 on ImageNet-1k** (Liu et al., 2022,
Table 1).

members.name: convnext_small_cls
members.signature: convnext_small_cls(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: ConvNeXtSmallWeights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.extended:
Combines the `convnext_small` backbone (`depths=(3, 3, 27, 3)`,
`dims=(96, 192, 384, 768)`) with a global average pool +
LayerNorm + linear classification head.  ~50M parameters.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
If `True`, loads ImageNet-1k pretrained weights when
available.  Defaults to `False`.

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description: Explicit weights enum member; takes precedence over `pretrained`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides on top of the canonical ConvNeXt-S config.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.convnext import convnext_small_cls
>>> model = convnext_small_cls(num_classes=1000)
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> model(x).logits.shape
(1, 1000)
Load ImageNet-pretrained weights:
>>> model = convnext_small_cls(pretrained=True)
>>> from lucid.models.weights import ConvNeXtSmallWeights
>>> model = convnext_small_cls(weights=ConvNeXtSmallWeights.IMAGENET1K_V1)
notes:
ConvNeXt-S reaches **83.1% top-1 on ImageNet-1k** (Liu et al.,
2022, Table 1).

members.name: convnext_base
members.extended:
Builds the canonical *ConvNeXt-B* configuration:
`depths=(3, 3, 27, 3)`, `dims=(128, 256, 512, 1024)`.  Final
feature width is 1024.  Approximately **89M parameters** — Swin-B
equivalent.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
If `True`, loads ImageNet-1k or ImageNet-22k pretrained
weights when available.  Defaults to `False`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides on top of the canonical ConvNeXt-B config.

returns.description:
A `ConvNeXt` backbone returning a flat $(B, 1024)$
feature.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.convnext import convnext_base
>>> model = convnext_base()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> model.forward_features(x).shape
(1, 1024)
notes:
ConvNeXt-B reaches **83.8% top-1 on ImageNet-1k** (224x224) and
**85.8% top-1** at 384x384 after ImageNet-22k pretraining (Liu
et al., 2022, Tables 1 and 11).

members.name: convnext_base_cls
members.signature: convnext_base_cls(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: ConvNeXtBaseWeights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.extended:
Combines the `convnext_base` backbone (`depths=(3, 3, 27, 3)`,
`dims=(128, 256, 512, 1024)`) with a global average pool +
LayerNorm + linear classification head.  ~89M parameters.

parameters.name: pretrained

members.name: convnext_large
members.extended:
Builds the canonical *ConvNeXt-L* configuration:
`depths=(3, 3, 27, 3)`, `dims=(192, 384, 768, 1536)`.  Final
feature width is 1536.  Approximately **198M parameters** — Swin-L
equivalent.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
If `True`, loads ImageNet-22k pretrained weights when
available.  Defaults to `False`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides on top of the canonical ConvNeXt-L config.

returns.description:
A `ConvNeXt` backbone returning a flat $(B, 1536)$
feature.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.convnext import convnext_large
>>> model = convnext_large()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> model.forward_features(x).shape
(1, 1536)
notes:
ConvNeXt-L reaches **84.3% top-1 on ImageNet-1k** (224x224) and
**86.6% top-1** at 384x384 after ImageNet-22k pretraining (Liu
et al., 2022, Tables 1 and 11).

members.signature: convnext_large_cls(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: ConvNeXtLargeWeights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.extended:
Combines the `convnext_large` backbone (`depths=(3, 3, 27, 3)`,
`dims=(192, 384, 768, 1536)`) with a global average pool +
LayerNorm + linear classification head.  ~198M parameters.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
If `True`, loads ImageNet-22k pretrained weights when
available.  Defaults to `False`.

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description: Explicit weights enum member; takes precedence over `pretrained`.

parameters.name: **overrides

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.convnext import convnext_large_cls
>>> model = convnext_large_cls(num_classes=1000)
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> model(x).logits.shape
(1, 1000)
Load ImageNet-pretrained weights:
>>> model = convnext_large_cls(pretrained=True)
>>> from lucid.models.weights import ConvNeXtLargeWeights
>>> model = convnext_large_cls(weights=ConvNeXtLargeWeights.IMAGENET1K_V1)

ConvNeXt-L reaches **84.3% top-1 on ImageNet-1k** (224x224) and

et al., 2022, Tables 1 and 11).

members.name: convnext_xlarge
members.extended:
Builds the canonical *ConvNeXt-XL* configuration:
`depths=(3, 3, 27, 3)`, `dims=(256, 512, 1024, 2048)`.  Final
feature width is 2048 — twice the ConvNeXt-B width.  Approximately
**350M parameters** — the largest variant in the paper.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
If `True`, loads ImageNet-22k pretrained weights when
available.  Defaults to `False`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides on top of the canonical ConvNeXt-XL config.

returns.description:
A `ConvNeXt` backbone returning a flat $(B, 2048)$
feature.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.convnext import convnext_xlarge
>>> model = convnext_xlarge()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> model.forward_features(x).shape
(1, 2048)
notes:
ConvNeXt-XL reaches **87.0% top-1 on ImageNet-1k** at 384x384
fine-tune resolution after ImageNet-22k pretraining (Liu et al.,
2022, Table 11) — the headline result of the paper.

members.name: convnext_xlarge_cls
members.signature: convnext_xlarge_cls(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: ConvNeXtXLargeWeights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.extended:
Combines the `convnext_xlarge` backbone (`depths=(3, 3, 27, 3)`,
`dims=(256, 512, 1024, 2048)`) with a global average pool +
LayerNorm + linear classification head.  ~350M parameters — the
largest ConvNeXt variant in the paper.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
If `True`, loads ImageNet-22k pretrained weights when
available.  Defaults to `False`.

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description: Explicit weights enum member; takes precedence over `pretrained`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides on top of the canonical ConvNeXt-XL config.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.convnext import convnext_xlarge_cls
>>> model = convnext_xlarge_cls(num_classes=1000)
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> model(x).logits.shape
(1, 1000)
Load ImageNet-22k-pretrained-then-1k-finetuned weights:
>>> model = convnext_xlarge_cls(pretrained=True)
>>> from lucid.models.weights import ConvNeXtXLargeWeights
>>> model = convnext_xlarge_cls(weights=ConvNeXtXLargeWeights.FB_IN22K_FT_IN1K)
notes:
ConvNeXt-XL reaches **87.0% top-1 on ImageNet-1k** at 384x384
fine-tune resolution after ImageNet-22k pretraining (Liu et al.,
2022, Table 11) — the headline result of the paper.

members.name: ConvNeXtTinyWeights
members.extended:
Currently ships `IMAGENET1K_V1` — torchvision's ImageNet-1k
checkpoint (acc@1 = 82.520 / acc@5 = 96.146) re-hosted under
`huggingface.co/lucid-dl/convnext-tiny`.

members.name: ConvNeXtSmallWeights
members.extended:
Currently ships `IMAGENET1K_V1` — torchvision's ImageNet-1k
checkpoint (acc@1 = 83.616 / acc@5 = 96.650).

members.name: ConvNeXtBaseWeights
members.extended:
Currently ships `IMAGENET1K_V1` — torchvision's ImageNet-1k
checkpoint (acc@1 = 84.062 / acc@5 = 96.870).

members.name: ConvNeXtLargeWeights
members.extended:
Currently ships `IMAGENET1K_V1` — torchvision's ImageNet-1k
checkpoint (acc@1 = 84.414 / acc@5 = 96.976).

members.name: ConvNeXtXLargeWeights
members.extended:
Currently ships `FB_IN22K_FT_IN1K` — Facebook AI Research's
ImageNet-22k pretraining → ImageNet-1k finetune checkpoint
(paper Table 11, acc@1 ≈ 87.0%), sourced via timm's
`convnext_xlarge.fb_in22k_ft_in1k` and re-hosted under
`huggingface.co/lucid-dl/convnext-xlarge`.  torchvision does not
publish a 1k-class ConvNeXt-XLarge head.

citation: Liu, Zhuang, et al. "A ConvNet for the 2020s." Proceedings of the IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition, 2022, pp. 11976-11986.
theory:
ConvNeXt is a *pure convolutional* architecture obtained by
systematically *modernizing* a ResNet using design choices borrowed
from the Swin Transformer.  Starting from a ResNet-50 the authors
apply a one-at-a-time roadmap: change the stage compute ratio to
$(3, 3, 9, 3)$, swap the stem for a non-overlapping
$4 \times 4$ patchify convolution, replace the bottleneck
with a depthwise $7 \times 7$ convolution followed by an
inverted bottleneck, move the activation and normalization to a
*single* GELU + LayerNorm after the depthwise conv, and replace
BatchNorm with LayerNorm.  The resulting block is



$$
\mathrm{Block}(x) = x + \gamma \odot \mathrm{Linear}_{4d \to d}\!\bigl(
    \mathrm{GELU}\!\bigl(\mathrm{Linear}_{d \to 4d}\!\bigl(
    \mathrm{LN}(\mathrm{DWConv}_{7 \times 7}(x))\bigr)\bigr)\bigr),
$$

where $\gamma \in \mathbb{R}^d$ is a *layer-scale* parameter
initialized to $10^{-6}$ (Touvron et al., 2021).

The pyramid follows the four-stage Swin layout — channel widths
$(C, 2C, 4C, 8C)$ with downsampling between stages — and the
canonical variants Tiny / Small / Base / Large / XLarge scale the
stem width $C$ and the depth of stage 3 from $9$ to
$27$.  The paper's headline result is that the modernised
ConvNet matches or exceeds Swin's ImageNet accuracy at the same
FLOPs while remaining a simple all-convolutional design.
